_comment: Season rules — thresholds, mechanics, or benchmarks tied to Set 17 balance (level timings, HP curves, comp/board benchmarks, the Realm of Gods mechanic, augment pool). Re-review patch_sensitivity:high entries every balance patch; regenerate the whole file when a new set replaces Set 17. Set-agnostic mechanics (interest math, streak gold, bench management) live in data/core/rules.core.json instead.

patch_meta._comment: Tracks which Data Dragon version these thresholds were last manually verified against. null means never verified — run `npm run check:patch` after reviewing patch_sensitivity:high/medium rules and it will report the current live version to fill in here.

rules.human_readable_name: Late Level 6 — Not Reaching Level 6 by Round 3-2
rules.short_summary: Player failed to reach level 6 by round 3-2, missing the primary early stabilization window.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You were still level {{actual_level}} at round 3-2! Most players aim to hit level 6 by this point. Level 6 gives you better shop odds and a 6th board slot.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 3-2 — behind the standard level 6 checkpoint. Late level 6 delays your stabilization window.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 3-2 (target: 6). Gold available: {{gold_available}}g. Assess whether a streak or augment decision consumed those resources.

rules.human_readable_name: Late Level 7 — Not Reaching Level 7 by Round 4-1
rules.short_summary: Player failed to reach level 7 by round 4-1, delaying access to 3-cost-dominant shop odds.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You were level {{actual_level}} at round 4-1, but most players aim for level 7 here. Level 7 gives much better units in the shop.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 4-1 (target: 7). You missed the standard level-7 stabilization window. Gold at 4-1: {{gold_at_4_1}}g.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 4-1 (benchmark: 7). Confirm reroll vs. standard intent was clear before stage 3-1.

rules.human_readable_name: Fast 8 Execution Delay — Level 8 Not Hit by Round 4-2
rules.short_summary: Player had sufficient gold but failed to reach level 8 by round 4-2.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You had {{gold_at_4_1}}g at round 4-1 but were only level {{actual_level}} at 4-2. Aim to hit level 8 around this time — press E to buy XP.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 4-2 with {{gold_at_4_1}}g available. Fast 8 window missed — buying XP at 4-1 should have gotten you to 8.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 4-2 despite {{gold_at_4_1}}g at 4-1. Was the roll-down yield enough to justify delaying level 8?

rules.human_readable_name: Late Level 9 — Failing to Reach Level 9 While HP-Stable
rules.short_summary: Player remained at level 8 through stage 5+ with sufficient gold and HP to push to level 9.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You were level {{actual_level}} at round 5-2 with {{gold_at_5_1}}g and {{hp_at_5_1}} HP. Pushing to level 9 unlocks 5-cost units!
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Still level {{actual_level}} at 5-2 with {{gold_at_5_1}}g and {{hp_at_5_1}} HP — the resources were there to push 9. Level 9 adds 15% 5-cost odds.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Level {{actual_level}} at 5-2 with {{gold_at_5_1}}g / {{hp_at_5_1}} HP. The 5-cost access from level 9 was the logical next investment.

rules.human_readable_name: Over-Rolling — Excessive Spend at Wrong Level for Carry Cost
rules.short_summary: Player rolled heavily (15+g) at a level where their intended carry cost tier has low shop odds.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You spent {{gold_spent_rolling}}g rolling at level {{player_level}}, but your main units only have a {{odds}}% chance to appear at that level!
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{gold_spent_rolling}}g at level {{player_level}} for {{carry_cost}}-cost units ({{odds}}% odds). That gold would have been better spent leveling to {{target_level}}.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Roll inefficiency: {{gold_spent_rolling}}g at level {{player_level}} for {{carry_cost}}-cost target ({{odds}}% per slot). Level {{target_level}} gives {{target_odds}}%.

rules.human_readable_name: Stage 2 Excessive HP Bleed
rules.short_summary: Player lost more than 15 HP during stage 2, indicating a critically weak early board.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You lost {{hp_lost}} HP during Stage 2 — that's a lot! Try to get 2-star units early or find a trait synergy to stabilize before Stage 3.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{hp_lost}} HP lost in Stage 2 (benchmark: <15). This typically indicates a weak opener or missing key synergy units.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Stage 2 HP loss: {{hp_lost}} (expected < 12 for a functional opener). Was this an opener issue, augment miss, or contested pool problem?

rules.human_readable_name: Stage 3 Critical HP Threshold
rules.short_summary: Player ended stage 3 below 60 HP, indicating dangerous bleed through the mid-game.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You only had {{hp}} HP entering Stage 4! That's dangerously low. Roll to upgrade your team and start winning fights.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{hp}} HP entering Stage 4 (safe zone: 60+). You lost {{hp_lost}} HP in Stage 3. Priority at 4-1: roll down to stabilize.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Stage 4 entry HP: {{hp}} (critical: <60). Stage 3 bleed: {{hp_lost}} HP. Damage formula: 6 base + surviving units.

rules.human_readable_name: Stage 5 Lethal Zone — Below 25 HP Entering Stage 5
rules.short_summary: Player entered stage 5 with 25 or fewer HP — a single full loss likely results in elimination.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You entered Stage 5 with only {{hp}} HP — one bad fight and you're out! Roll harder in Stage 4 to win fights and preserve HP.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{hp}} HP at Stage 5 entry — lethal range. Stage 5 losses deal 10+ damage; one full loss eliminates you. This traces back to Stage 4 stabilization.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Stage 5 entry HP: {{hp}} (lethal zone: <=25). Stage 4 bleed: {{hp_lost}}. The stabilization window at 4-1/4-2 was the last chance.

rules.human_readable_name: Carry Item Misallocation — Carry Items on Tank Units
rules.short_summary: Player placed two or more damage-oriented items on a tank-role unit.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: Your tank unit {{tank}} had damage items equipped! Put those items on your main carry unit instead.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{tank}} holding damage items: {{items}}. Tanks benefit from defensive items — those damage items would boost your carry significantly.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Carry item misallocation: {{items}} on {{tank}} (role: tank). Was there a carry available to hold these at that point?

rules.human_readable_name: Board Strength Lag — Unit Cost Sum Below Stage Benchmark
rules.short_summary: Player's total board unit cost sum was below the stage-appropriate benchmark.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: Your board was worth about {{cost_sum}}g at Stage {{stage}}, but most players aim for around {{benchmark}}g at this point.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Board cost sum: {{cost_sum}} vs. Stage {{stage}} benchmark (~{{benchmark}}). Your board was {{deficit}}g below average power level — this correlates with HP lost this stage.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Board cost proxy: {{cost_sum}} (Stage {{stage}} expected: ~{{benchmark}}). Deficit: {{deficit}}. Supplement with trait activation count for fuller picture.

rules.human_readable_name: God Round Misalignment — No God Chosen Twice Across Stages 2-4, 3-4, 4-4
rules.short_summary: Player chose three different gods across the three God rounds, missing the God Blessing at stage 4-7.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You picked a different God each round — you missed your special God Blessing at round 4-7! Pick the same God at least twice to unlock their powerful bonus.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: No God alignment achieved. Picks: {{picks}}. Missing the 4-7 Blessing is a significant mid-game power spike loss. Even if one offering was better short-term, alignment value outweighs it.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: God alignment failure: {{picks}}. Max picks of any one god: {{max_count}}. Evaluate: was the alternative god's immediate offering worth more than the lost Blessing?

rules.human_readable_name: Evelynn Offering HP Risk — Accepting HP-Cost Offering Below 40 HP
rules.short_summary: Player accepted an Evelynn God offering that costs HP while already below 40 HP.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You chose an Evelynn offering that cost HP when you only had {{hp}} HP left! At low HP, consider a safer God option.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Evelynn HP offering accepted at {{hp}} HP (-{{cost}} HP). Below 40 HP, HP expenditure on God offerings is extremely risky — a single bad Stage 4 fight could eliminate you.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Evelynn HP offering: -{{cost}} HP at {{hp}} HP. Stage 4 average damage per loss: ~12-18. Was the offering value above the survivability delta?

rules.human_readable_name: God Alignment Locked Out — Not Aligned After Stage 3-4
rules.short_summary: Player made only one God pick through stage 3-4 with only 4-4 remaining.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: After Round 3-4, you still hadn't picked the same God twice! Round 4-4 is your last chance — pick the same God you chose earlier to unlock their blessing at 4-7!
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Unaligned after Stage 3-4: picks were {{picks}}. Round 4-4 is your last chance. Pick {{best_god}} again to secure the blessing.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Alignment gap post-3-4: {{picks}}. 4-4 is the correction window. Best alignment candidate: {{best_god}} ({{best_count}} pick(s)).

rules.human_readable_name: Economy Augment Chosen During HP Crisis
rules.short_summary: Player picked an economy-category augment (from this set's augment pool) while below 45 HP.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You picked {{augment}}, an economy augment, at only {{hp}} HP! Below 45 HP a combat or item augment usually helps more right away.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{augment}} (econ) picked at {{hp}} HP, below the 45 HP safe threshold. Econ augments pay off over time you may not have at this HP.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Econ augment {{augment}} taken at {{hp}} HP. Compare immediate board-power alternatives against the multi-round econ payoff given elimination risk.

rules.human_readable_name: Missing Augment Data — Coverage Gap
rules.short_summary: No augment picks were captured for a bottom-4 finish, so augment decisions could not be coached.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: We couldn't see your augment picks this game, so we skipped augment coaching.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: Augment picks weren't captured for this match. This usually happens on very short games or a GEP registration delay.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Augment telemetry gap: 0 picks recorded, placement {{placement}}. Check GEP registration timing for early-elimination games.

rules.human_readable_name: No Frontline Unit at Stage 4+
rules.short_summary: Player's board had no tank-role unit (per this set's champions.json role tags) for 2+ consecutive stage-4+ PvP rounds.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: Your board had no tank for {{rounds}} fights in a row at Stage 4+! Add a frontline unit so your carry survives long enough to deal damage.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: No tank-role unit for {{rounds}} consecutive Stage 4+ fights (since {{since}}). {{hint}}
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Frontline gap: {{rounds}} rounds without a tank-role unit since {{since}}. {{hint}} Even a cheap tank buys several seconds of combat time for carries to fire.

rules.human_readable_name: Below Season Econ-Benchmark Curve
rules.short_summary: Player's gold at a curated checkpoint round (data/sets/set{N}/econ-benchmarks.json) was below the set's expected minimum.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: At {{round}} you had {{gold}}g, but {{benchmark}}g is the target for this point in the game. {{notes}}
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{round}} checkpoint: {{gold}}g vs {{benchmark}}g benchmark ({{deficit}}g short). {{notes}}
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Econ curve deficit at {{round}}: {{deficit}}g below benchmark. {{notes}}

rules.human_readable_name: Naked Level — XP Bought Without a Gold or Board Cushion
rules.short_summary: Player bought XP leaving fewer than 10g remaining without a stable 2-starred core (fewer than 3 two-star units) to justify the risk.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You bought XP at {{round}} and had only {{gold_after}}g left with just {{two_stars}} two-star unit(s)! That's risky if you lose the next fight.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: XP purchase at {{round}} left {{gold_after}}g and {{two_stars}} two-star unit(s) — not enough cushion to absorb a loss.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Naked level at {{round}}: {{gold_after}}g remaining, {{two_stars}} two-star units. Evaluate whether the level spike was worth the exposed gold/board position.

rules.human_readable_name: Off-BiS Final Carry Build
rules.short_summary: Player's main carry finished the match with 3+ completed items where at least one falls outside the set-curated BiS/alt list for that champion (data/sets/set{N}/carry-bis.json), and no picked augment excuses the deviation.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: Your {{carry}} finished with {{off_bis_count}} item(s) that aren't the best fit! The ideal build is {{bis_items}}.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{carry}} carried {{off_bis_count}} off-BiS item(s). Curated build: {{bis_items}}. Off-kit items lose synergy damage.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: Off-BiS build on {{carry}}: {{off_bis_count}} item(s) outside {{bis_items}}. Check whether an augment this match should have been logged as an excuse.

rules.human_readable_name: Stuck One Unit Short of a Trait Breakpoint
rules.short_summary: Player was exactly one unit away from the next trait tier (data/sets/set{N}/trait-breakpoints.json) for 3+ consecutive PvP rounds without completing it.

coaching_message_templates.low_rank: You were one unit away from {{trait}} {{next_tier}} for {{rounds}} fights in a row! Grab one more {{trait}} unit to unlock it.
coaching_message_templates.mid_rank: {{trait}} sat at one unit short of tier {{next_tier}} for {{rounds}} consecutive fights. One more unit activates it for every remaining fight.
coaching_message_templates.high_rank: {{trait}} breakpoint gap: one unit short of {{next_tier}} for {{rounds}} rounds. Free efficiency left on the table — scout for the cheapest available holder.
